name: Representation Lens
summary: Compare how votes become seats, how seats become power, and how institutions translate public preference into governing leverage.
whenToUseIt: Use this when analysing elections, party strategy, Senate leverage, or whether a result actually creates a working mandate.
steps: Map which chamber determines government formation and which shapes review power.
Identify party discipline, crossbench leverage, and preference flow effects.
Note what the result changes for governing strategy.
Ask whether public interpretation of the result matches institutional reality.
example: A party can win government in the House yet still require extensive Senate negotiation, which means the public story of victory overstates the executive freedom available.
